description:
Auto-Extract Action Items from Meetings into Kanban

When a new meeting is ingested via the Krisp webhook, automatically trigger an AI pipeline that extracts action items from the transcript and key points, persists them to the database, and creates corresponding Kanban cards on the user's configured board — eliminating the need for manual extraction on the meeting details page.

Requirements:

Webhook Trigger & Pipeline Entry Point
- Extend the existing Krisp webhook ingestion handler to invoke the action item extraction pipeline immediately after a meeting is saved
- Ensure idempotency: if the webhook fires more than once for the same meeting, do not duplicate action items or Kanban cards
- Queue or defer the AI extraction step if necessary to avoid blocking the webhook response (target < 500ms webhook acknowledgement)

AI Extraction
- Reuse the existing extraction logic currently exposed via the manual "Extract Action Items" button on the meeting details page — refactor it into a shared service function callable from both the webhook handler and the UI
- Pass the full transcript and key points to the AI (claude-sonnet-4) to identify action items
- For each extracted action item, capture: title, description, assignee (if determinable from speaker/participant data), due date (if mentioned), and priority (inferred from language urgency)
- If no transcript is available yet at webhook time (e.g., processing delay), schedule a retry or trigger extraction when the transcript becomes available

Database Persistence
- Save extracted action items to the existing action items table, linked to the source meeting ID and the authenticated user
- Mark each record with an extraction source field (e.g., auto_webhook) to distinguish from manually extracted items
- Set initial status to open

Kanban Card Creation
- For each persisted action item, create a Kanban card on the board configured in the user's Krisp meeting integration settings
- Place new cards in a sensible default column (e.g., "To Do" or the first column of the configured board)
- Populate card fields from action item data: title, description, due date, priority
- Link the Kanban card back to the source action item and meeting (for traceability)

Deduplication & Conflict Handling
- Before creating a Kanban card, check if a card already exists for the same action item (by meeting ID + action item fingerprint) to handle retries and manual extractions that may have already run
- If a user has already manually extracted action items for a meeting, skip auto-creation for that meeting or merge gracefully without duplication

UI / UX Updates
- Update the meeting details page to reflect auto-extracted action items, showing their linked Kanban cards with status
- Adjust or relabel the manual "Extract Action Items" button to "Re-extract Action Items" (or hide it) if auto-extraction has already run for that meeting
- Show a non-blocking notification or meeting detail badge (e.g., "3 action items auto-added to Kanban") confirming successful auto-extraction

Edge Cases to Consider
- Meeting arrives with no transcript (audio-only or processing lag) — handle gracefully with a retry mechanism or fallback to key points only
- Krisp integration is configured but no Kanban board is selected — log a warning, save action items to the database, and surface a prompt in the UI to configure a board
- AI returns zero action items — record a completed extraction attempt with zero results so retries do not re-run unnecessarily
- Partial failure (action items saved but Kanban card creation fails) — ensure the action item record is preserved and card creation can be retried independently

Dependencies & Prerequisites
- Krisp webhook integration must be active and correctly storing meeting data
- User must have a Kanban board configured in Krisp integration settings
- Existing manual extraction logic must be successfully refactored into a reusable service before wiring up the automated trigger

summary:
## Summary: Auto-Extract Action Items from Meetings into Kanban

### Changes Implemented
- **Database schema**: Added `extraction_source` column to `action_items` table (varchar, default `'manual'`) to distinguish auto-webhook vs manual extraction
- **Migration**: Created `0011_add-extraction-source.sql` with the column addition and indexes for efficient querying (extraction source index + composite meeting/user index for deduplication)
- **Extraction service**: Updated `extractActionItemsForMeeting()` to accept a `source` parameter (`"auto_webhook"` | `"manual"`), saved to each action item record
- **Improved idempotency**: Extraction check now filters out soft-deleted items (`isNull(deletedAt)`), so re-extraction works correctly after items are deleted
- **Card deduplication**: Existing `cardId` check prevents duplicate cards; added "Meeting" tag (indigo) to auto-created cards for traceability
- **Webhook handler**: Now passes `"auto_webhook"` as extraction source; logs warning when user has no default board configured
- **Manual extraction route**: Passes `"manual"` as extraction source
- **Action items API**: Returns `extractionSource` field in GET response
- **TypeScript types**: Added `extractionSource: string` to `ActionItem` interface
- **Meeting Detail Drawer UI**:
  - Auto-extraction badge: green banner showing "N action items auto-extracted · M added to Kanban" when auto-webhook items exist
  - Re-labeled button: "Re-extract Action Items" instead of just "Re-extract" for clarity
  - "On Kanban" indicator: icon + label on action items linked to cards
  - "Auto-extracted" badge: green text on auto-webhook items
  - No-board prompt: amber text "Select a board to create Kanban cards" when items exist without cards and no board is selected

### Files Modified
- `lib/db/schema.ts` - Added `extractionSource` column to actionItems table
- `types/index.ts` - Added `extractionSource` field to ActionItem interface
- `lib/actions/extractActionItems.ts` - Added source parameter, improved idempotency with deletedAt check, added Meeting card tag
- `app/api/webhooks/key-points/route.ts` - Pass "auto_webhook" source, no-board warning
- `app/api/action-items/extract/route.ts` - Pass "manual" source
- `app/api/action-items/route.ts` - Include extractionSource in query select
- `components/meeting/MeetingDetailDrawer.tsx` - Auto-extraction badge, improved action item display
- `drizzle/migrations/0011_add-extraction-source.sql` - New migration
- `drizzle/migrations/meta/_journal.json` - Migration journal entry

### Notes for Developer
- Run `DATABASE_URL=<url> npx drizzle-kit push` or apply migration `0011_add-extraction-source.sql` to add the new column
- Existing action items will default to `extraction_source = 'manual'`
- The auto-extraction pipeline was already wired up in the webhook handler; this enhancement adds source tracking, better deduplication, and UI feedback
- Card deduplication relies on the existing `cardId` foreign key check (skip if already linked) plus the idempotency check (skip if action items already exist for meeting)
